title: 3D Geometry
description: Explore lines, planes, and surfaces in three-dimensional space using vector methods.

contentBlocks.text:
A line in 3D space can be defined by a point $\mathbf{a}$ and a direction vector $\mathbf{d}$: 

$$\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a} + t\mathbf{d}$$
The scalar equation of a plane is $ax + by + cz = d$, where $\mathbf{n} = \langle a, b, c \rangle$ is the normal vector.
